html:
[1]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 钦定四库全书 - Imperially approved Siku Quanshu, an official compilation of Chinese classics commissioned by the Qing dynasty.
  • 子部十二 - Twelfth category of the Siku Quanshu, the 'Miscellaneous' section.
  • 归潜志 - Title of the work, meaning 'Records of Returning to Seclusion.'
  • 杂事之属 - Subcategory 'Miscellaneous Events.'
  • 提要 - Summary or introduction to a work, providing background information and key points.
  • 御史从益之子 - Liu Qi's father, Liu Congyi, who was a censor (御史).
  • 太学生 - Students at the Imperial Academy.
  • 举进士不第 - Failed to pass the imperial examination for the jinshi degree.
  • 元兵入汴 - Mongol invasion of Kaifeng, the capital of the Jin dynasty.
  • 遁还乡里 - Fled back to his hometown.
  • 戊戌复出 - Year 1238 (Wu Xu) when Liu Qi reappeared in public life.
  • 魁南京 - Became the top scholar in the examination held in Kaifeng.
  • 选充山西东路考试官 - Selected to serve as an examiner in Shanxi Province.
  • 从征南行省 - Followed the Southern Campaign of the Yuan dynasty.
  • 辟置幕府 - Appointed to the military staff, often as an advisor or administrator.
  • 殁 - Died, often used in formal or literary contexts.
  • 旧以金史载之文艺传 - Previously included in the 'Biographies of Scholars and Artists' section of the Jin History.
  • 归潜 - Retreated into seclusion after the fall of the Jin dynasty.
  • 壬辰北还 - Year 1232 (Ren Chen) when Liu Qi returned to the north.
  • 乙未自序 - Year 1235 (Yi Wei) when Liu Qi wrote his preface.
  • 昔所闻见 - What I have heard and seen in the past.
  • 暇日记忆 - In my spare time, I remember.
  • 随得随书 - Writing down whatever comes to mind.
  • 录大梁事纪 - Records of events in Daliang (Kaifeng).
  • 哀宗亡国始末 - Beginning and end of the fall of the Jin dynasty under Emperor Aizong.
  • 录崔立碑事 - Records of the events surrounding the erection of a stele by Cui Li.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 钦定四库全书 - One of the largest collections of Chinese literature, compiled during the Qing dynasty.
  • 子部十二 - The 'Miscellaneous' section of the Siku Quanshu includes works that do not fit neatly into other categories.
  • 归潜志 - A collection of observations and reflections on life, politics, and society by Liu Qi.
  • 杂事之属 - Includes records of miscellaneous events and anecdotes.
  • 提要 - A summary or introduction to a work, often providing background information and key points.
  • 御史 - A high-ranking official responsible for oversight and supervision in the imperial government.
  • 太学生 - Students at the Imperial Academy, often future officials.
  • 举进士 - Passing the imperial examination for the jinshi degree, the highest degree leading to a career in government.
  • 元兵入汴 - The Mongol invasion of Kaifeng, marking the fall of the Jin dynasty.
  • 遁还乡里 - Fleeing back to one's hometown due to political or military upheaval.
  • 戊戌 - The year 1238, during the reign of Emperor Shizu of the Yuan dynasty.
  • 魁南京 - Becoming the top scholar in the imperial examination held in Kaifeng.
  • 选充山西东路考试官 - Selected to serve as an examiner in Shanxi Province.
  • 从征南行省 - Following the Southern Campaign of the Yuan dynasty.
  • 辟置幕府 - Appointed to the military staff, often as an advisor or administrator.
  • 殁 - Death, often used in formal or literary contexts.
  • 旧以金史载之文艺传 - The Jin History is a historical record of the Jin dynasty, and the 'Biographies of Scholars and Artists' section includes notable figures.
  • 归潜 - Retreating into seclusion, often after a period of political or military involvement.
  • 壬辰 - The year 1232, during the reign of Emperor Shizu of the Yuan dynasty.
  • 乙未 - The year 1235, during the reign of Emperor Shizu of the Yuan dynasty.
  • 昔所闻见 - Refers to past experiences and observations.
  • 暇日记忆 - Refers to the act of recalling and writing down memories in one's spare time.
  • 随得随书 - Writing down thoughts and observations as they come to mind.
  • 录大梁事纪 - Records of events in Daliang (Kaifeng), the capital of the Jin dynasty.
  • 哀宗亡国始末 - The beginning and end of the fall of the Jin dynasty under Emperor Aizong.
  • 录崔立碑事 - Records of the events surrounding the erection of a stele by Cui Li, a notable figure during the Jin dynasty.

Proper Nouns

  • 钦定四库全书 - Official title of the Siku Quanshu.
  • 子部十二 - Twelfth category of the Siku Quanshu.
  • 归潜志 - Title of Liu Qi's work.
  • 杂事之属 - Subcategory 'Miscellaneous Events.'
  • 提要 - Title of the summary or introduction.
  • 御史从益之子 - Liu Qi's father, Liu Congyi.
  • 太学生 - Students at the Imperial Academy.
  • 举进士 - Imperial examination for the jinshi degree.

[2]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 立碑以媚之 - A phrase meaning 'to erect a stele to flatter [someone],' indicating an act of sycophancy.
  • 刼祁使撰文 - A phrase meaning 'forced Qi to write an article,' suggesting coercion.
  • 辨亡 - A title or theme meaning 'On the Fall' or 'On the Decline,' likely referring to a historical or philosophical text.
  • 雜記 - A term meaning 'miscellaneous notes' or 'random records,' indicating a collection of diverse entries.
  • 語録之軆 - A phrase meaning 'the form of a sayings collection,' referring to a type of literary work.
  • 殊不相 - A phrase meaning 'do not match at all' or 'are not consistent,' indicating a lack of coherence.
  • 疑此二篇本自為一卷 - A phrase meaning 'suspect that these two pieces originally formed a single volume,' suggesting a hypothesis about the text's structure.
  • 殿全书之末 - A phrase meaning 'to conclude the entire book,' indicating a position in the text.
  • 別以语録为第十三卷 - A phrase meaning 'separately, to list a sayings collection as the thirteenth volume,' indicating a reorganization of the text.
  • 附缀于后 - A phrase meaning 'to append to the end,' indicating an addition to the text.
  • 因萹页不均 - A phrase meaning 'due to uneven page distribution,' suggesting a practical reason for reorganization.
  • 割语録之半移缀此卷 - A phrase meaning 'to cut the sayings collection in half and move it to this volume,' indicating a specific action taken.
  • 体例参差 - A phrase meaning 'inconsistent format,' indicating a lack of uniformity.
  • 壬辰之变 - A phrase meaning 'the great change of Renchen year,' referring to a significant historical event.
  • 目击事状 - A phrase meaning 'to witness the events firsthand,' indicating direct observation.
  • 纪载胥得其实 - A phrase meaning 'all records are accurate,' indicating the reliability of the accounts.
  • 金史本 - A phrase meaning 'the official history of Jin,' referring to a historical record.
  • 称祁此志于金末之事多有足征 - A phrase meaning 'states that Qi's account of the late Jin period is well-documented,' indicating the historical value of the text.
  • 哀宗本纪 - A phrase meaning 'the biography of Emperor Aizong,' referring to a specific section of the historical record.
  • 全以所言为据 - A phrase meaning 'completely based on what is said,' indicating reliance on the text.
  • 义若大金国志 - A phrase meaning 'if the *Da Jin Guo Zhi* (History of the Great Jin),' referring to another historical text.
  • 此志不书出降 - A phrase meaning 'this account does not record the surrender,' indicating a discrepancy.
  • 与金史相合 - A phrase meaning 'consistent with the Jin history,' indicating agreement.
  • 可证大金国志之误 - A phrase meaning 'can prove the error in the *Da Jin Guo Zhi*,' indicating a correction.
  • 元史 - A phrase meaning 'the official history of the Yuan,' referring to a historical record.
  • 称壬辰正月太宗自白波济河而南 - A phrase meaning 'states that in the first month of Renchen year, the Emperor Tai Zong crossed the Yellow River from Baipo and moved south,' indicating a historical event.
  • 睿 - A term meaning 'wise' or 'enlightened,' often used as a title or descriptor for emperors.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 廷臣 - Refers to court officials.
  • 祁 - Refers to Qi, likely a historical figure.
  • 金 - Refers to the Jin dynasty.
  • 元 - Refers to the Yuan dynasty.
  • 太宗 - Refers to the Emperor Tai Zong of the Yuan dynasty.
  • 白波 - Refers to Baipo, a location in the Yellow River region.
  • 金史 - Refers to the official history of the Jin dynasty.
  • 大金国志 - Refers to the *Da Jin Guo Zhi* (History of the Great Jin), another historical text.
  • 元史 - Refers to the official history of the Yuan dynasty.
  • 壬辰 - Refers to the Renchen year, a specific year in the Chinese calendar.
  • 哀宗 - Refers to Emperor Aizong of the Jin dynasty.
  • 伊喇蒲阿 - Refers to Yila Pu'a, a historical figure.
  • 睿 - Refers to the Emperor Tai Zong of the Yuan dynasty.

Proper Nouns

  • 廷臣 - Court officials.
  • 祁 - Qi, a historical figure.
  • 金 - The Jin dynasty.
  • 元 - The Yuan dynasty.
  • 太宗 - The Emperor Tai Zong of the Yuan dynasty.
  • 白波 - Baipo, a location in the Yellow River region.
  • 金史 - The official history of the Jin dynasty.
  • 大金国志 - The *Da Jin Guo Zhi* (History of the Great Jin).
  • 元史 - The official history of the Yuan dynasty.
  • 壬辰 - The Renchen year.
  • 哀宗 - Emperor Aizong of the Jin dynasty.
  • 伊喇蒲阿 - Yila Pu'a, a historical figure.
  • 睿 - The Emperor Tai Zong of the Yuan dynasty.

[3]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 宗由峭石滩涉汉而北以渡河 - Describes a journey involving crossing the Han River from a steep stone shoal to the north.
  • 涉汉同在一时 - Indicates that the crossing of the Han River happened at the same time.
  • 此志则载睿宗涉汉在辛卯十一月 - States that the record mentions Emperor Taizong crossing the Han River in the eleventh month of the year 辛卯 (Xin Mao).
  • 太宗渡河乃在壬辰 - States that Emperor Taizong crossed the river in the year 壬辰 (Ren Chen).
  • 可证元史之误 - Suggests that the consistency can prove errors in the Yuan Dynasty history.
  • 又如载天兴元年刘元规使北朝不知所终 - Provides an example of a person who was sent to the Northern Court and whose fate is unknown.
  • 而金史不纪不书其事 - Indicates that the Jin Dynasty history does not record this event.
  • 载萨克苏媒蘖李元妃本纪不着其名 - States that the biography of Li Yuanyi does not mention the name of Sa Kesu.
  • 载大定十七年三月朔诸国使臣朝见遇雨放朝 - Describes an event where ambassadors from various countries paid homage to the court, which was suspended due to rain.
  • 与周煇北辕録合 - Indicates that this event is consistent with the records in Zhou Hui's collection.
  • 本纪但载十六年三月朔日蚀放朝一条 - States that the official biography only records one entry about the court being suspended due to a solar eclipse.
  • 载金代钞法凡八易其名 - Describes the paper money system in the Jin Dynasty, which changed its name eight times.
  • 而金史食货志失载通货改为通寳通寳又改为通货一条 - Indicates that the chapter on finance and economy in the Jin Dynasty history does not record the change from currency to tongbao and back to currency.
  • 皆足以补正史之阙 - Suggests that all these records can supplement the gaps in the official history.
  • 至于金史交聘表称大定十六年宋汤彦充申请使 - As for, the diplomatic correspondence table in the Jin Dynasty history records that in the sixteenth year of Dading, Song Dynasty's Tang Yan served as an envoy.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 睿宗 - Refers to Emperor Taizong of the Tang Dynasty.
  • 太宗 - Another title for Emperor Taizong of the Tang Dynasty.
  • 金史 - The official history of the Jin Dynasty.
  • 姚燧 - A prominent scholar and official during the Yuan Dynasty.
  • 牧庵集 - A collection of writings by Yao Sui.
  • 苏天爵 - A historian and official during the Yuan Dynasty.
  • 元史 - The official history of the Yuan Dynasty.
  • 天兴元年 - A specific year during the Jin Dynasty.
  • 刘元规 - A person who served as an envoy to the Northern Court.
  • 萨克苏 - A person who promoted or incited actions.
  • 李元妃 - A person whose name is not mentioned in the biography.
  • 大定十七年 - A specific year during the Jin Dynasty.
  • 周煇 - A person who wrote a collection of writings.
  • 北辕録 - A collection of writings by Zhou Hui.
  • 通货 - Currency in the Jin Dynasty.
  • 通寳 - A type of coin in the Jin Dynasty.
  • 食货志 - A chapter in the Jin Dynasty history about finance and economy.
  • 交聘表 - A diplomatic correspondence table in the Jin Dynasty history.
  • 宋 - Refers to the Song Dynasty.
  • 汤彦 - A person who served as an envoy during the Jin Dynasty.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • This category contains notes on syntactic ambiguities in the source text, which are not specific to any single term or expression.

[4]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 祈请使 - A term for envoy or diplomatic representative.
  • 此志书其事于正大八年 - Indicates that this record was written in the eighth year of the Zhèngdà era.
  • 遂战殁 - Indicates that they died in battle.
  • 壬辰离编 - A historical text or compilation.
  • 金史 - The official history of the Jin Dynasty.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 天兴元年 - The first year of the Tianxing era, a period in the Jin Dynasty.
  • 正大八年 - The eighth year of the Zhèngdà era, another period in the Jin Dynasty.
  • 完顔思烈 - A historical figure from the Jin Dynasty.
  • 王渥 - A historical figure from the Jin Dynasty.
  • 持嘉喀齐喀英 - A historical figure from the Jin Dynasty.
  • 霸州 - A historical location in the Jin Dynasty.
  • 潞郭阿林 - A historical figure from the Jin Dynasty.
  • 宋兵 - Refers to the troops of the Southern Song Dynasty.
  • 师安石 - A historical figure from the Jin Dynasty.
  • 恭校上 - Indicates that the text was respectfully submitted for examination.

Proper Nouns

  • 图克坦乌登 - A historical figure.
  • 完顔思烈 - A historical figure.
  • 王渥 - A historical figure.
  • 持嘉喀齐喀英 - A historical figure.
  • 潞郭阿林 - A historical figure.
  • 师安石 - A historical figure.
  • 元好问 - A historical figure and scholar.
  • 壬辰离编 - A historical text or compilation.
  • 金史 - The official history of the Jin Dynasty.
  • 郭朝釪 - A historical figure and scholar.
  • 王恽 - A historical figure and scholar.
  • 陆孟鳬 - A historical figure and scholar.
  • 钱曾 - A historical figure and scholar.

[5]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 弱冠 - The age of twenty, traditionally marked by the ceremonial cap, symbolizing the transition to adulthood.
  • 太皥之墟 - The ruins of Taihao, referring to the ancient abode of the legendary Emperor Taihao, symbolizing a place of origin or ancestral roots.
  • 干戈流落 - Wandering amidst war and strife, describing the hardships of living through conflict and displacement.
  • 烟消灰灭 - Smoke dissipates and ashes vanish, a metaphor for the complete disappearance of something, often used to describe the fading of power or glory.
  • 一布衣 - A commoner, referring to someone of low social status, often used to describe oneself in a humble manner.
  • 完归 - Return intact, meaning to return home safely or to return to one's original state.
  • 不幸之幸 - A misfortune that is also a blessing, a paradoxical phrase that describes a situation where a negative event leads to a positive outcome.
  • 被攻刼之苦 - The suffering of being attacked and plundered, referring to the hardships endured during times of conflict and invasion.
  • 奔走之劳 - The toil of constant travel, describing the physical and mental strain of frequent movement.
  • 饭疏饮水 - Eating coarse food and drinking water, a phrase that describes a simple and austere lifestyle.
  • 蒂诸胷臆 - To harbor in one's heart, meaning to keep something in mind or to be preoccupied with it.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 总纂官 - Chief compiler, referring to the official in charge of compiling a text.
  • 总校官 - Chief proofreader, referring to the official responsible for overseeing the proofreading of a text.
  • 归潜志 - Record of Returning to Seclusion, a title that suggests a personal or historical account of retreat or reflection.
  • 先子 - My father, a respectful term used to refer to one's deceased father.
  • 行宫 - Imperial residence, indicating the political context.
  • 有司 - Officials, referring to the bureaucracy.
  • 甲午 - Jia Wu, indicating the year.
  • 劝戒规鉴 - Admonition and guidance, emphasizing the importance of the teachings.

[6]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 钦定四库全书 - Title of a monumental imperial compilation of Chinese literature, the *Complete Library of the Four Treasuries*, commissioned by the Qing emperor Qianlong.
  • 归潜志卷一 - Title of a historical and biographical text, *Gui Qian Zhi*, meaning *Records of Returning to Obscurity*, Volume One.
  • 元 刘祁 撰 - Indicates that the text was compiled by Liu Qi of the Yuan dynasty. '撰' means 'compiled' or 'authored.'
  • 金海陵庶人 - Refers to the Jurchen ruler of the Jin dynasty, Wanyan Liang (also known as Emperor Xizong), who was the second emperor of the Jin. '庶人' is a term used to denote his status as a commoner before ascending the throne or as a posthumous label.
  • 读书有文才 - A concise phrase meaning 'read books and had literary talent.'
  • 尝书人扇 - A classical expression meaning 'once wrote on a fan for someone,' referring to a literary or poetic act.
  • 大柄若在手 - A metaphor meaning 'if the power were in one’s hand.'
  • 清风满天下 - A poetic expression meaning 'the clear wind would fill the world,' implying the spread of virtue or influence.
  • 人知其大志 - 'People knew of his great ambition.'
  • 正隆南征 - Refers to the southern campaign of Emperor Wanyan Liang during his reign, known as the *Zhenglong* era.
  • 屯兵百万西湖上 - 'Stationing a hundred thousand troops on the western lake (West Lake in Hangzhou).'
  • 宣孝太子 - The title of the son of Emperor Shizong of Jin, who was later posthumously honored as the 'Xuanxiao Prince' and given the posthumous title of 'Xianzong' (显宗).
  • 世宗子章宗父也 - 'Son of Emperor Shizong, father of Emperor Zhangzong.'
  • 追谥显宗 - 'Posthumously given the title of Xianzong (显宗).'
  • 好文学 - 'Loved literature.'
  • 作诗善画人物马尤工 - 'Wrote poetry and was skilled in painting, especially people and horses.'
  • 迄今人间多有存者 - 'To this day, many of these works remain in the world.'
  • 天资聪悟 - 'Had a naturally bright and quick mind.'
  • 诗词多有可称者 - 'His poems and verses are often praised.'
  • 宫中绝句 - 'A quatrain from the palace.'
  • 五云金碧拱朝霞 - A poetic line, possibly describing the splendor of a palace or imperial court.
  • 楼阁峥嵘帝子家 - 'Towering pavilions and halls of the imperial prince’s residence.'
  • 三十六宫 - 'Thirty-six palaces,' a poetic reference to the imperial palace complex.
  • 尽卷东风无处不杨花 - A poetic line, likely referring to the wind carrying willow fluff (杨花) throughout the palace.
  • 真帝王诗也 - 'Truly the poetry of an emperor.'
  • 命翰林待制朱澜侍夜饮 - 'Ordered Zhu Lan, a翰林待制 (a literary official), to serve during a night drinking session.'
  • 夜饮何所乐 - 'What is the joy of a night drink?'
  • 三杯淡醽醁 - 'Three cups of light, pure wine (醽醁 is a refined wine).'
  • 一曲冷琵琶 - 'A single cold (or quiet) tune of the pipa (a Chinese lute).'
  • 夜深灯欲花 - 'At night, the lamp’s light resembles a flower.'
  • 陶陶复陶陶 - A poetic repetition, meaning 'drunk and merry, again and again.'
  • 醉乡岂有涯 - 'The land of drunkenness has no bounds.'
  • 聚骨扇词 - 'A ci poem titled *Jubone Fan* (possibly a title of a fan or a person's name).'
  • 巧様翻腾叠作湘波皱 - 'Skillfully shaped, the folds resemble the ripples of the Xiang River.'
  • 金镂小钿花草鬭翠绦 - 'Gold inlaid with small floral decorations, competing with green ribbons.'
  • 更结同心绶 - 'Further tied with a *tongxin shou* (a ribbon symbolizing a married couple’s union).'
  • 金殿日长承宴久 - 'In the golden hall, the feast lasted long.'
  • 招来暂喜清风透 - 'Inviting in a temporary joy of the clear wind.'
  • 轻轻褪入香罗袖 - 'Gently slipping into the fragrant silk sleeve.'
  • 又擘橙为软金杯 - 'Cutting an orange into a soft golden cup.'
  • 词风流紫府郎 - 'A ci poem by a graceful official of the Purple Palace (possibly a court title).'
  • 痛饮乌纱岸 - 'Drinking deeply while leaning on the black hat (a reference to a scholar-official's hat).'
  • 冷怯玻瓈盌 - 'Cold and timid, the glass bowl.'
  • 纤纤白玉葱 - 'Thin and white, like the green onion (a metaphor for the fingers).'
  • 分破黄金弹 - 'Breaking apart the golden ball (a metaphor for splitting a fruit or a poetic image).'
  • 借得洞庭春 - 'Borrowing the spring of Dongting Lake.'
  • 飞上桃花面 - 'Flying onto the face of peach blossoms.'
  • 尝为铁券行数十韵 - 'Once composed a poem of dozens of lines about the iron covenant (a type of imperial guarantee).'
  • 笔力甚雄 - 'The brushwork was very powerful.'
  • 又有送张建致仕归吊王庭筠下世诗 - 'Also composed a poem to send Zhang Jian back to retirement and to mourn Wang Tingyun’s passing.'
  • 具载飞龙记中 - 'Fully recorded in *Feilong Ji* (The Record of the Flying Dragon), a historical text.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 钦定四库全书 - *The Complete Library of the Four Treasuries* (*Siku Quanshu*), a massive imperial literary compilation completed in the Qing dynasty, considered one of the greatest works of classical Chinese literature.
  • 归潜志 - *Gui Qian Zhi* (Records of Returning to Obscurity), a historical and biographical text by Liu Qi of the Yuan dynasty, chronicling the lives of Jin and Yuan dynasty figures.
  • 金海陵庶人 - Wanyan Liang (1122–1150), the second emperor of the Jin dynasty, known for his tyranny and expansionist policies. The term *shushen* (庶人) may be a posthumous label or a way of referring to his early life as a commoner.
  • 正隆 - The era name of Wanyan Liang, during which he launched a major southern campaign against the Southern Song dynasty.
  • 宣孝太子 - A son of Emperor Shizong of Jin, who later became Emperor Zhangzong. He was posthumously honored with the title *Xuanxiao* (宣孝) and *Xianzong* (显宗).
  • 世宗 - Emperor Shizong of Jin, a well-regarded ruler known for his military and cultural achievements.

Proper Nouns

  • 钦定四库全书 - Proper title of a monumental imperial compilation of Chinese literature, the *Complete Library of the Four Treasuries*, commissioned by the Qing emperor Qianlong.
  • 归潜志卷一 - Proper title of a historical and biographical text, *Gui Qian Zhi*, meaning *Records of Returning to Obscurity*, Volume One.
  • 元 刘祁 撰 - Proper name of the compiler, Liu Qi of the Yuan dynasty.
  • 金海陵庶人 - Proper name of the Jurchen ruler of the Jin dynasty, Wanyan Liang (also known as Emperor Xizong).
  • 宣孝太子 - Proper title of the son of Emperor Shizong of Jin, who later became Emperor Zhangzong.
  • 世宗子章宗父也 - Proper title of Emperor Shizong of Jin, father of Emperor Zhangzong.
  • 追谥显宗 - Proper title of the posthumous honor *Xianzong* (显宗).
  • 正隆 - Proper era name of Emperor Wanyan Liang of the Jin dynasty.
  • 章宗 - Proper title of Emperor Zhangzong of Jin.

[7]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 好文善歌诗 - Fond of literature and skilled in singing poetry.
  • 萧然琴书满案 - Sparse and simple, with musical instruments and books covering the table.
  • 萧然 - Sparse or simple.
  • 琴书 - Musical instruments and books.
  • 满案 - Covering the table.
  • 诸子环侍 - Children surrounding and serving.
  • 无俗谈 - Without mundane conversation.
  • 可谓贤公子矣 - Can be called a virtuous noble son.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 元裕之李长源王飞伯皆游其门 - Indicates that Yuan Yuzhi, Li Changyuan, and Wang Feibo were all disciples of the same teacher or belonged to the same school.
  • 飞伯尝有诗云 - Suggests that Wang Feibo once composed a poem.
  • 宣平坊里榆林巷便是临淄公子家 - A poetic reference to a specific location, likely a metaphorical or symbolic representation of a noble family's residence.
  • 寂寞画堂豪贵少时容词客聴琵琶 - Describes a scene where a noble family's hall is quiet, with few wealthy people, and wordsmiths listen to the sound of a lute.
  • 天兴初北兵犯河南 - Refers to the early period of the Tianxing era when northern forces invaded Henan.
  • 公已卧疾 - Indicates that the person in question was already ill.
  • 敌势如此不能支止可以降心从顺全吾祖宗如得完颜氏一族归我国中使女直不灭则善矣 - A complex sentence expressing the idea that given the enemy's strength, the only way to survive is to submit, ensuring the safety of one's ancestors and the preservation of the Wanyan clan.
  • 后数月薨 - Indicates that the person died several months later.
  • 五子防曰守禧字庆之年少亦有俊才作诗与字画亦可喜状貌白晳丰神秀彻如仙人公特钟爱尝防予指其书画曰将以付斯人 - Describes the eldest son, Shouxi, who is young, talented, and favored by his father.
  • 甚有唐人逺意 - Suggests that the poem has a distant, classical Chinese flavor.
  • 不可谓无志者也 - Implies that despite the circumstances, the person still had aspirations.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 古今諸家學 - Refers to the various schools of thought throughout history.
  • 晩年書大進 - Indicates significant progress in writing during later years.
  • 詩専法唐人 - Means that poetry was exclusively modeled after Tang Dynasty poets.
  • 魁然一时文士领袖 - Describes someone as the outstanding leader among scholars and writers of their time.
  • 爵位士大夫罕及焉 - Indicates that such a high rank among officials was rarely achieved by others.
  • 性疎旷无机凿 - Describes a person as having a broad and unpretentious nature, free from petty concerns.
  • 治民镇静不生事 - Means governing with calmness and avoiding unnecessary actions.
  • 在朝循循无异言 - Describes behavior in court as cautious and without causing controversy.
  • 家居未尝有声色之娱 - Indicates that at home, there were no indulgences in music, dance, or other forms of entertainment.
  • 夫人卒不再娶 - States that after the wife's death, he did not remarry.
  • 断荤肉粗衣粝食不恤也 - Means abstaining from meat, wearing coarse clothing, and eating simple food, showing no concern for personal comfort.
  • 酷好学至老不衰 - Indicates a strong passion for learning that continued until old age.
  • 上至六经解外及浮屠庄老医药丹诀无不究心 - Means studying deeply not only the Six Classics but also Buddhism, Laozi, Zhuangzi, medicine, and alchemy.
  • 其所着有太解老子解南华指要滏水集外集无虑数十万言 - Refers to his works, including commentaries and collections, totaling tens of thousands of words.

  • 浮屠 - A term for Buddhism.

  • 西京 - Refers to the ancient capital of Chang'an, the modern Xi'an.
  • 益都府 - Refers to Yidu, an ancient administrative region.
  • 左氏春秋 - Refers to the *Zuo Zhuan*, an important historical and literary text.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 擢髙第 - Literally 'promoted to a high rank,' referring to being selected for a high position after examination or merit.
  • 為文法莊周左氏 - 'In writing, he followed the style of Zhuangzi and Zuo Zhuan,' indicating literary influence.
  • 其辭雄竒 - 'His words were bold and strange' or 'his style was powerful and strange.'
  • 簡古人 - 'Simplified the ancients' or 'made the style of the ancients more concise.'
  • 皆宗之 - 'All revered him' or 'were all followers of his style.'
  • 文風由此一變 - 'The literary style thus changed completely' or 'the literary trend changed from this point.'
  • 慨然有經世心 - 'He was filled with a sense of concern for state affairs' or 'he had ambitions to reform the world.'
  • 泰和南犯 - 'The southern incursion of Tahe' — likely refers to a military incursion from the south, possibly by a neighboring state.
  • 兩上疏防其勝負 - 'He submitted two memorials to prevent or predict the outcome.'
  • 章宗咨異 - 'Zhongzong [Emperor] consulted him on unusual matters' — *章宗* is likely a dynastic title or emperor's name.
  • 給送軍中 - 'Sent to the military' or 'sent to the front lines.'
  • 後多如所料 - 'Later, many things turned out as he had predicted.'
  • 宰執竒其文 - 'The ministers found his writing strange' or 'the officials were surprised by his writing.'
  • 荐入翰林 - 'Recommended to enter the Hanlin Academy.'
  • 北方兵起 - 'When the northern army rose' or 'when the northern forces began to attack.'
  • 不報 - 'Not responded to' or 'his memorial was not accepted.'
  • 宣宗南渡 - 'Emperor Xuanzong fled south' — refers to a dynastic move to the south during a crisis.
  • 連知貢舉 - 'Consecutively presided over the imperial examinations.'
  • 逾新格 - 'Exceeded the new regulations' or 'broke the new standards.'
  • 出倅坊州 - 'Was sent as an assistant governor of Fangzhou.'
  • 未赴改京兆府判官 - 'Before going, he was reassigned to be a judge in Jingzhao Prefecture.'
  • 卒於南京 - 'Died in Nanjing.'
  • 公为人聪敏于学无所不通 - 'The gentleman was intelligent and learned all things.'
  • 少自负其才 - 'In his youth, he was proud of his talent.'
  • 谓功名可俯拾 - 'He said that fame and official rank could be easily attained.'
  • 作矮柏赋 - 'Wrote an essay on the short cypress' — a symbolic or allegorical work.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 莊周 - Zhuangzi, a Daoist philosopher and writer from the Warring States period, known for his allegorical and philosophical style.
  • 左氏 - Zuo Qiuming, the author of the *Zuo Zhuan*, a historical commentary on the *Spring and Autumn Annals*, known for its narrative style and historical detail.
  • 泰和 - Likely refers to a place or event, possibly a northern incursion or a military campaign. Could also be a dynastic name or a title.
  • 章宗 - Likely refers to an emperor of the Jin Dynasty, Emperor Zhangzong (1189–1208), who ruled during the late Jin period.
  • 翰林 - The Hanlin Academy, a prestigious scholarly institution in imperial China, responsible for literary and governmental duties.
  • 宣宗 - Emperor Xuanzong of the Jin Dynasty, who fled south during a crisis, possibly the Mongol invasions.
  • 南京 - Likely refers to the southern capital of the Jin Dynasty, which may have been Kaifeng or another city in the south.
  • 珠格 - A name of a high-ranking official in the Jin Dynasty. The note indicates that it was previously written as *術虎* and later changed to *珠格*.
  • 左司都事 - A bureaucratic position, likely within the central government, responsible for administrative duties.
  • 正大末 - 'End of the Zhengda era' — a reign title of Emperor Wanyan Sheng of the Jin Dynasty.
  • 取人逾新格 - 'Selected people beyond the new standards' — refers to the imperial examination system and the selection of candidates.
  • 倅 - Assistant governor or deputy official.
  • 坊州 - A historical prefecture in northern China.
  • 京兆府 - A major administrative region, likely referring to the capital or a central government office.
  • 矮柏赋 - A literary work, possibly symbolic of resilience or modesty.
  • 诸葛孔明 - Zhuge Liang, a famous strategist of the Three Kingdoms period.

  • 翰林 - Hanlin Academy

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 由小官上万言书援宋为证甚切当路者以迂阔见抑 - A complex phrase describing a low-ranking official submitting a lengthy memorial to the court, using Song dynasty precedents to support his argument, which was criticized by those in power for being impractical.
  • 士论惜之 - Means 'scholars lamented this,' indicating the reaction of the scholarly community.
  • 中年度其道不行益纵酒自放无仕进意 - Means 'in his middle age, he realized that his way would not be followed and thus increasingly indulged in drinking, letting go of his restraint, and having no intention of pursuing official career advancement.'
  • 得官未尝成考旋即归居闲与禅僧士子游惟以文酒为事啸歌袒裼出礼法外 - Means 'when he obtained an official position, he never achieved any notable success, soon returned home, lived in leisure, associated with Buddhist monks and scholars, and engaged only in literary and drinking activities, singing and laughing, and behaving in a way that went beyond the bounds of propriety.'
  • 或饮数月不醒人有酒见招不择贵贱必往往辄醉虽沈醉亦未尝废著书 - Means 'sometimes he drank for several months without waking up; if invited to a drinking party, he did not choose between high or low status individuals, and he would often get drunk; even when deeply drunk, he never stopped writing.'
  • 至于谈笑怒骂灿然皆成文理 - Means 'even in his joking, laughing, anger, and cursing, everything was clearly expressed in a coherent manner.'
  • 天资喜士后进有一善极口称推一时名士皆由公显于世 - Means 'by nature, he loved scholars, and for any good qualities in young scholars, he would praise them to the utmost, and many famous scholars of his time became known through his promotion.'
  • 又与之拍肩尔汝忘年齿相懽教育抚摩恩若亲戚故士大夫归附号为当世龙门尝自作屏山居士末曰惟喜推借后进如周嗣明张瑴李经王权雷渊余先子姓名【刘从益】宋九嘉皆以 - Means 'he would also treat them as equals, addressing them by their given names, forgetting age differences, and enjoying their company, educating and nurturing them with the affection of a close relative, so many officials and scholars flocked to him, calling him the 'Dragon Gate' of the contemporary era. He once wrote the biography of 'Screen Mountain Hermit,' concluding with 'I only love to promote young scholars, such as Zhou Siming, Zhang Jue, Li Jing, Wang Quan, Lei Yuan, my father's name [Liu Congyi], Song Jiujia, etc., who all became prominent.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 宋 - Refers to the Song dynasty, a period known for its cultural and intellectual achievements.
  • 龙门 - A metaphorical term for a prestigious place or person who can promote talented individuals, derived from the legend of the Dragon Gate where fish transform into dragons.
  • 屏山居士 - A self-designated title meaning 'Screen Mountain Hermit,' indicating a reclusive and scholarly lifestyle.
  • 周嗣明张瑴李经王权雷渊余先子姓名【刘从益】宋九嘉 - Names of notable scholars and figures of the time, including the author's father Liu Congyi.

Proper Nouns

  • 屏山居士 - A self-designated title.
  • 周嗣明张瑴李经王权雷渊余先子姓名【刘从益】宋九嘉 - Names of notable scholars and figures.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 兄呼而居士使酒玩世人 - Refers to a recluse who enjoys wine and social interaction.
  • 忤其意辄嫚骂之 - Indicates the recluse's temper; if someone offends his mood, he will insult them.
  • 其自赞曰 - Introduces a self-description.
  • 躯榦短小而芥视九州 - A metaphor comparing his small stature to the vastness of the world.
  • 形容寝陋而蚁虱公侯 - Describes his physical appearance as unattractive but compares it to the nobility of ants and lice.
  • 言语蹇吃而连环可解 - Indicates his eloquence despite his stutter; his speech is hesitant but can still be understood.
  • 笔札讹痴而挽回万牛 - Describes his writing as seemingly foolish but capable of moving great things.
  • 宁为时所弃不为名所囚 - Expresses his preference for freedom over recognition; rather than be abandoned by the times, he would not be imprisoned by fame.
  • 吾所学者浄名庄周 - Refers to his philosophical influences; what I have studied is the teachings of the Buddha and Zhuangzi.
  • 其余应物文字如碑志诗赋号外藁 - Refers to his practical writings; the rest of his writings, such as inscriptions, epitaphs, poems, and essays, are called outer drafts.
  • 拟庄子内外篇 - Indicates the structure of his writings; modeled after the inner and outer chapters of Zhuangzi.
  • 解楞严金刚经老子庄子 - Refers to his commentaries; he expounded on the *Lengyan Sutra*, the *Diamond Sutra*, *Laozi*, and *Zhuangzi*.
  • 又有中庸集解鸣道集解号为中国心学西方文教数十万言 - Refers to his extensive commentaries; he also wrote comprehensive commentaries on *The Doctrine of the Mean* and *The Way of Enlightenment*, which are called Chinese heart learning and Western teachings, totaling tens of thousands of words.
  • 尝曰自庄周后惟王绩元结郑厚与吾此其所学也 - Indicates his view on the continuity of his philosophical lineage; he once said that since Zhuangzi, only Wang Ji, Yuan Jie, Zheng Hou, and himself have studied this.
  • 或谈儒释异同 - Indicates his philosophical interests; sometimes he would discuss the differences between Confucianism and Buddhism.
  • 虽环而攻之莫能屈世岂复有此俊杰人哉 - Indicates his intellectual prowess and uniqueness; even if surrounded and attacked, no one could defeat him; is there still such a talented person in the world?

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 雷翰林渊字希顔 - Standard biographical formula: 'Lei Hanlin Yuan, style name Xiyuan' (Lei Hanlin is a title, not a surname; '字' is the style name).
  • 与余同里闬且姻家也 - 'Shared the same neighborhood and were also related by marriage,' a common way to express close personal ties.
  • 父思西仲名进士 - 'His father, Si Xizhong, was named jinshi' — '名进士' is a title indicating someone who passed the imperial examination.
  • 仕至同知北京转运司 - 'Served up to the position of co-official in the Beijing Transport Commission' — '同知' is a mid-level official title.
  • 注易行于世 - 'Commented on the *Yijing* and had his work circulated in the world' — '注' is commentary, '行于世' is 'circulated in the world.'
  • 公防丧父 - 'His father died while he was serving as a public official' — '公防' is likely a typo or misreading of '公服' (in official attire) or '公事' (official duties).
  • 以孤童入太学 - 'As an orphaned child, he entered the Imperial Academy' — '孤童' is an orphaned child, '太学' is the Imperial Academy.
  • 虽贫甚不以介意 - 'Though he was very poor, he did not consider it a matter of concern' — '不以介意' is a set phrase meaning 'not to take it to heart.'
  • 从李屏山游遂知名 - 'Studied under Li Pingshan and thus became known' — '游' is a classical term for studying under a teacher or associate.
  • 调泾州录事 - 'Transferred to the position of recorder in Jingzhou' — '录事' is a minor official responsible for record-keeping.
  • 防死后改东平 - 'After the death of [the person involved], he was reassigned to Dongping' — '防' here is likely a miswriting of '防' or '防' as in 'after the death' or 'after the case was concluded.'
  • 迁东阿令 - 'Promoted to the position of magistrate of Dong’e.'
  • 授徐州观察判官 - 'Appointed as the judicial official of Xuzhou.'
  • 兴定末召为英王府文学 - 'At the end of the Xingding era, he was summoned to serve as a literary official in the Ying王府' — '兴定' is the era name of the Jin dynasty emperor, '文学' is a literary or literary official.
  • 俄入翰林为应奉 - 'Soon entered the Hanlin Academy as a Yingfeng' — '应奉' is a title for a Hanlin official.
  • 拜监察御史 - 'Was appointed as a Censorate Official' — '监察御史' is a title of an official in the Censorate.
  • 言五事称防 - 'Submitted five memorials, which were praised' — '称防' is possibly a miswriting of '称善' (praised) or '称颂' (commended).
  • 又弹劾不避贵臣 - 'Also impeached without fear of powerful ministers.'
  • 出巡郡邑所至有威誉 - 'When touring the prefectures and counties, he was known for his authority and reputation.'
  • 凡奸豪不法者立棰杀之 - 'All criminals and lawbreakers among the powerful were executed on the spot with the whip' — '立棰杀之' is a strong phrase indicating swift and decisive punishment.
  • 坐此为小人所讼罢去 - 'Because of this, he was accused by villains and dismissed from office.'
  • 久之起为太学博士 - 'After some time, he was reappointed as a Doctor of the Imperial Academy.'
  • 南京转运司户籍判官 - 'Served as the official in charge of household registration in the Nanjing Transport Commission.'
  • 迁翰林修撰 - 'Promoted to the position of Hanlin compiler.'
  • 一夕暴卒年四十八 - 'One night he died suddenly at the age of forty-eight.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 应州浑源人 - He was from Huyuan, Yingshou (modern-day Shanxi province).
  • 同知北京转运司 - A mid-level official in the Beijing Transport Commission, a key administrative body during the Jin dynasty.
  • 注易行于世 - Commented on the *Yijing* (Book of Changes), a classical Confucian text.
  • 太学 - The Imperial Academy, the highest educational institution in imperial China.
  • 李屏山 - Li Pingshan, likely a scholar or teacher of the time.
  • 录事 - A minor official responsible for administrative and record-keeping duties.
  • 兴定 - An era name of the Jin dynasty emperor Wanyan Liang (1214–1216).
  • 英王府 - The Prince Ying’s mansion, indicating a court position.
  • 监察御史 - A censors’ official in the imperial administration, responsible for monitoring officials.
  • 称防 - Likely a miswriting of '称善' (praised) or '称颂' (commended).
  • 弹劾不避贵臣 - Impeaching without fear of powerful ministers, a rare and significant act in the Jin court.
  • 太学博士 - A title in the Imperial Academy, responsible for teaching and commentary on classical texts.
  • 南京转运司 - The Transport Commission in Nanjing, a key administrative and fiscal body.
  • 翰林修撰 - A Hanlin compiler, responsible for compiling and editing official texts.
  • 暴卒 - Sudden death, possibly due to illness or overwork.
  • 元好问雷希顔墓铭 - Yuan Haowen’s *Tombstone Inscription for Lei Xiyuan*, a literary work by the famous Jin dynasty poet and historian.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The sentence '雷翰林渊字希顔应州浑源人与余同里闬且姻家也' is a long biographical formula, combining multiple clauses. It should be parsed as: 'Lei Hanlin Yuan, style name Xiyuan, of Huyuan, Yingshou, shared the same neighborhood and was also related by marriage with me.'
  • The sentence '父思西仲名进士仕至同知北京转运司注易行于世' is a list of the father's achievements: 'His father, Si Xizhong, was named jinshi, served up to the position of co-official in the Beijing Transport Commission, and commented on the *Yijing*, which was circulated in the world.'
  • The phrase '公防丧父' is unclear, but likely a miswriting of '公事' (public duty) or '公服' (official attire), suggesting that while he was in service, his father died, and he became an orphan.
  • '游' here is a classical term for studying under or associating with a teacher or mentor.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 麻九畴字知防 - The name and courtesy name of the individual, which should be rendered as 'Ma Jiuchou, courtesy name Zhifang.'
  • 初名文纯 - Indicates his original name was 'Wen Chun,' which can be translated as 'originally named Wen Chun.'
  • 易州人 - Refers to his place of origin, 'Yizhou person,' which can be translated as 'from Yizhou.'
  • 防颖悟善草书能诗号神童 - Describes his early talents and reputation. '防颖悟' means 'Ma was insightful and quick-witted,' '善草书' means 'skilled in cursive script,' '能诗' means 'capable of poetry,' and '号神童' means 'known as a child prodigy.'
  • 既长入太学刻苦自厉 - Describes his later studies and self-discipline. '既长' means 'after he grew up,' '入太学' means 'entered the Imperial Academy,' '刻苦自厉' means 'worked hard and disciplined himself.'
  • 为赵闲闲李屏山所知 - Indicates that he was known to Zhao Xianxian and Li Pingshan. '为...所知' means 'was known to.'
  • 南渡后居郾蔡间入遂平西山读书为经义学精甚 - Describes his activities after the Southern Migration. '南渡后' means 'after the Southern Migration,' '居郾蔡间' means 'lived in Yancheng and Caizhou,' '入遂平西山读书' means 'entered the West Mountain of Suiping to study,' '为经义学精甚' means 'excelled in the study of Confucian classics.'
  • 兴定末试开封府词赋乙经义魁再试南省复然声誉大振 - Describes his achievements in the imperial examinations. '兴定末' means 'at the end of the Xingding era,' '试开封府词赋乙经义魁' means 'won first place in the poetry and prose section of the Kaifeng Prefecture examination,' '再试南省复然' means 'passed the provincial examination again,' '声誉大振' means 'his reputation greatly increased.'
  • 南都妇人小儿皆知名 - Indicates that his fame was widespread. '南都' means 'the Southern Capital,' '妇人小儿皆知名' means 'even women and children knew of him.'
  • 及廷试以误绌士论惜之 - Describes his performance in the final imperial examination. '及廷试' means 'when he took the final imperial examination,' '以误绌' means 'due to a mistake, he failed,' '士论惜之' means 'scholars lamented it.'
  • 已而居不为科举计 - Describes his subsequent actions. '已而' means 'afterward,' '居' means 'retired to live in seclusion,' '不为科举计' means 'did not pursue the imperial examinations.'
  • 正大初门人王説王采苓俱中第 - Describes the success of his disciples. '正大初' means 'in the early Zhengda era,' '门人' means 'disciples,' '王説王采苓俱中第' means 'Wang Shuo and Wang Cailing both passed the examination.'
  • 上以其年防怪而问之且知知防为师近臣言其有才学 - Describes the emperor's inquiry. '上' means 'the emperor,' '以其年防怪而问之' means 'was surprised by his young age and asked about him,' '且知知防为师近臣言其有才学' means 'and knew that Zhifang was recommended by a close minister who spoke highly of his talent and learning.'
  • 平章政事侯公挚翰林学士赵公秉文俱荐之特召赐进士第以病不拜官告归病已赴调授太常寺太祝俄入翰林后以病去 - Describes his appointments and resignations. '平章政事侯公挚翰林学士赵公秉文俱荐之' means 'were recommended by Pingzhangzhengshi Hou Gongzhi and Hanlin Xueshi Zhao Gongbingwen,' '特召赐进士第' means 'were specially summoned and granted the title of Jinshi,' '以病不拜官告归' means 'due to illness, he did not take office and returned home,' '病已赴调授太常寺太祝' means 'after recovering, he was appointed as Taichangshi Taizhu,' '俄入翰林后以病去' means 'soon entered the Hanlin Academy but left due to illness.'
  • 居郾久之北兵入河南知防挈其孥入确山避乱后复出为兵士所得驱之北边至广平病死知防 - Describes his final years. '居郾久之' means 'lived in Yancheng for a long time,' '北兵入河南' means 'northern soldiers invaded Henan,' '知防挈其孥入确山避乱' means 'Zhifang took his family to Queshan to avoid the chaos,' '后复出为兵士所得驱之北边至广平病死' means 'later he came out and was captured by the soldiers, driven north to Guangping, where he died of illness.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 易州 - A place name, modern-day Yizhou, Hebei Province.
  • 太学 - The Imperial Academy, the highest institution of learning in ancient China.
  • 赵闲闲李屏山 - Zhao Xianxian and Li Pingshan, likely high-ranking officials or scholars.
  • 南渡 - Refers to the Southern Migration, a significant event in Chinese history when the Song Dynasty moved its capital south due to the Jin invasion.
  • 开封府 - Kaifeng Prefecture, the capital of the Song Dynasty.
  • 南省 - Provincial examination, a part of the imperial examination system.
  • 南都 - The Southern Capital, another name for Kaifeng during the Southern Song period.
  • 兴定 - An era name of the Jin Dynasty.
  • 正大 - An era name of the Jin Dynasty.
  • 平章政事 - A high-ranking official position, equivalent to a chancellor.
  • 翰林学士 - A prestigious position in the Hanlin Academy, responsible for drafting imperial edicts and other official documents.
  • 太常寺 - The Imperial Sacrificial Office, responsible for religious ceremonies and rituals.
  • 太祝 - A position in the Imperial Sacrificial Office, responsible for conducting sacrifices.
  • 北兵 - Northern soldiers, referring to the invading forces from the north.
  • 确山 - Queshan, a place in Henan Province.
  • 广平 - Guangping, a place in Hebei Province.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • This phrase describes Ma Jiuchou's early talents and reputation. It can be read as 'Ma was insightful and quick-witted, skilled in cursive script, capable of poetry, and known as a child prodigy.'
  • This phrase describes his later studies and self-discipline. It can be read as 'after he grew up, he entered the Imperial Academy and worked hard to discipline himself.'
  • This phrase indicates that he was known to Zhao Xianxian and Li Pingshan. It can be read as 'he was known to Zhao Xianxian and Li Pingshan.'
  • This phrase describes his activities after the Southern Migration. It can be read as 'after the Southern Migration, he lived in Yancheng and Caizhou, entered the West Mountain of Suiping to study, and excelled in the study of Confucian classics.'
  • This phrase describes his achievements in the imperial examinations. It can be read as 'at the end of the Xingding era, he won first place in the poetry and prose section of the Kaifeng Prefecture examination, passed the provincial examination again, and his reputation greatly increased.'
  • This phrase indicates that his fame was widespread. It can be read as 'even women and children in the Southern Capital knew of him.'
  • This phrase describes his performance in the final imperial examination. It can be read as 'when he took the final imperial examination, he failed due to a mistake, and scholars lamented it.'
  • This phrase describes his subsequent actions. It can be read as 'afterward, he retired to live in seclusion and did not pursue the imperial examinations.'
  • This phrase describes the success of his disciples. It can be read as 'in the early Zhengda era, his disciples Wang Shuo and Wang Cailing both passed the examination.'
  • This phrase describes the emperor's inquiry. It can be read as 'the emperor was surprised by his young age and asked about him, and knew that Zhifang was recommended by a close minister who spoke highly of his talent and learning.'
  • This phrase describes his appointments and resignations. It can be read as 'were recommended by Pingzhangzhengshi Hou Gongzhi and Hanlin Xueshi Zhao Gongbingwen, were specially summoned and granted the title of Jinshi, due to illness, he did not take office and returned home, after recovering, he was appointed as Taichangshi Taizhu, soon entered the Hanlin Academy but left due to illness.'
  • This phrase describes his final years. It can be read as 'lived in Yancheng for a long time, northern soldiers invaded Henan, Zhifang took his family to Queshan to avoid the chaos, later he came out and was captured by the soldiers, driven north to Guangping, where he died of illness.'

'正大中' refers to the reign period of Emperor Wuzong of Jin, '先子' means 'my father,' '令叶' means 'in the county of Ye,' '复来游' means 'came again to visit,' '后归洛下' means 'later returned to Luoyang,' '病殁' means 'died of illness,' '有诗数千首' means 'had thousands of poems,' '常在行槖中' means 'always carried in his travel bag.'
  • 其佳句有云院静寛留月牕虚细度云又莺衔晩色啼深树燕掠春隂入短墙 - Lists some of the author's father's best lines. '其佳句有云' means 'his best lines are as follows,' '院静寛留月' means 'the courtyard is quiet and wide, the moon lingers,' '牕虚细度云' means 'the window is empty, thin clouds pass,' '又莺衔晩色啼深树' means 'again, the oriole carries the evening color, singing in the deep trees,' '燕掠春隂入短墙' means 'the swallows skim the spring shade, entering the short walls.'

  • 下距天兴三年凡五年 - Means 'five years later,' where '天兴三年' (Tianxing Year 3) refers to 1234, the year of the Jin dynasty's fall.
  • 汾卒之年当四十有三 - Indicates the age of the person (Fen) at death, which is calculated to be forty-three years old.
  • 哀哉 - An expression of sorrow or lamentation.
  • 平生诗甚多 - Refers to the large number of poems written by the person throughout their life.
  • 不自收集 - Means 'did not collect them himself,' indicating a lack of personal curation.
  • 往往散落 - Means 'often scattered,' suggesting that the poems were not preserved or organized.
  • 再过长安 - Refers to the second time the person passed through Chang'an (present-day Xi'an), a significant city in Chinese history.
  • 三辅楼台失归燕 - Describes a scene where the towers of the Three Auxiliaries (a region around Chang'an) lose their returning swallows, symbolizing desolation or loss.
  • 上林花木怨啼鹃 - Describes the flowers and trees in the imperial gardens (Shanglin) lamenting the cries of cuckoos, symbolizing sorrow or longing.
  • 空余一掬伤时泪 - Means 'leaving only a handful of tears for the times,' expressing deep sorrow.
  • 暗堕昭陵石马前 - Describes tears falling silently before the stone horses of the Zhaoling mausoleum, a reference to the tomb of Emperor Taizong of Tang.

  • 回头三十四年非 - Means 'looking back, thirty-four years have passed,' reflecting on the passage of time.
  • 东风万里衡门下 - Describes the east wind blowing far and wide, symbolizing change or movement.
  • 依旧中原一布衣 - Means 'still just a commoner in the Central Plains,' expressing a sense of unchanged status.
  • 望少室 - Refers to gazing at the Shaoshi mountain, a place of significance.
  • 圭影静涵秋气老 - Describes the shadow of a ritual tablet (gui) quietly absorbing the autumn atmosphere, symbolizing stillness and aging.

  • 案元好问礼部尚书赵公神道碑思文以贞祐三年达汴梁特授太府丞兴定二年陞同知西安军节度使并行六部郎中事四年始内除右司谏兼侍御史是思文初达汴梁未尝即为侍御史与此志 - Means 'According to Yuan Haowen's epitaph for Zhao Shangshu, Siwen was appointed as the Chief of the Imperial Household in Kaifeng in the third year of Zhenyou, and was promoted to the position of Deputy Governor of the Western Army in Xian'an in the second year of Xingding, and served as the Deputy Secretary of the Six Ministries. This indicates that Siwen did not immediately become the Censor-in-Chief when he first arrived in Kaifeng,' providing additional historical context.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 王翰林良臣字大用潞州人 - Standard biographical introduction in Classical Chinese, where '王翰林' indicates the person's position or title, '良臣' is his given name, '字大用' is his courtesy name, and '潞州人' indicates his place of origin.
  • 长于律诗尖新工对属 - Describes the person's literary skill, specifically in composing regulated verse (律诗) that is fresh (尖新) and skilled in antithesis (工对属).
  • 南渡在馆后从李天英北征遇害 - Describes the person's fate, indicating that after the Southern Migration (南渡), he followed Li Tianying on a northern campaign and was killed.
  • 笔底有神扶气力人间无处着声名 - A poetic expression praising the person's writing ability, suggesting that his writing has divine inspiration and strength, and that his reputation is widespread.
  • 流转年光桥下水翻腾时态岭头云溪翁道号竒聋子除却松风百不闻 - Uses natural imagery to convey a sense of the passage of time and the tranquility of the hermit's life. '流转年光' means 'the flowing years,' '桥下水' means 'water under the bridge,' '翻腾时态' means 'turbulent times,' '岭头云' means 'clouds on the mountain top,' '溪翁' means 'hermit by the stream,' '道号' means 'religious name,' '竒聋子' means 'strange deaf man,' and '除却松风百不闻' means 'except for the sound of the pine wind, nothing else is heard.'
  • 少有词赋声擢第读书为文有体致 - Describes the person's early literary achievements, indicating that he had a reputation for poetry and prose, passed the imperial examinations, and wrote with style and elegance.
  • 南渡为左司郎中坐事免久之为礼部侍郎司农太常卿翰林侍讲学士 - Describes the person's career, indicating that after the Southern Migration, he served as Left Censor (左司郎中), was dismissed due to some matter, and later served in various high-ranking positions.
  • 从末帝东征至蔡州贼陷死 - Describes the person's final fate, indicating that he followed the last emperor on an eastern campaign, reached Cai Prefecture, and was killed when the city fell to the enemy.
  • 有子嵩字企隆 - Introduces the person's son, indicating that he had a son named Song, with the courtesy name Qilong.

  • 尝属余作记 - 'Once asked me to compose a record'; classical way of expressing a request for a biographical or memorial text.
  • 余家三世交 - 'Our families have been acquainted for three generations'; reflects a long-standing familial or scholarly relationship.
  • 工书法 - 'Skilled in calligraphy'; standard way to describe a person's artistic or technical skill.
  • 赵黄山 - Possibly a nickname or honorific; may refer to Zhao Huangshan, a famous calligrapher or scholar.
  • 自号锦峯老人 - 'Self-styled as Jinfeng Lao Ren (Old Man of the Golden Peak)'; common way to refer to someone's self-chosen literary title or honorific.
  • 卒于京兆幕 - 'Died while serving in the capital's military or administrative office'; '京兆幕' refers to a position in the capital's administrative or military staff.
  • 从屏山诸公游 - 'Studied under the scholars of Pingshan'; '屏山' is likely a nickname or honorific for a scholar or teacher.
  • 为文以多为胜 - 'Wrote in abundance, considering quantity as superiority'; reflects a stylistic preference in classical Chinese literary circles.
  • 从军数年 - 'Served in the military for several years'; common way to describe a period of military service.
  • 入为省掾 - 'Was appointed to a provincial office'; '掾' refers to an assistant or official in a government office.
  • 大理司直 - A title of an official in the imperial court, specifically in the Department of Justice (大理寺).
  • 自号无着道人 - 'Self-styled as Wu Zhuo Dao Ren (the Unattached Daoist)'; common way to adopt a Daoist or philosophical title.
  • 屏山为作梦记 - 'Pingshan composed a Dream Record (Mengji) about him'; refers to a biographical or memorial text.
  • 予先子同年进士也 - 'My late father and he were fellow jinshi (imperial examination graduates)'; '同年' refers to those who passed the same examination in the same year.
  • 贞祐五年经义魁也 - 'In the fifth year of the Zhenyou era, he was the top scorer in the jingyi examination'; '魁' means 'champion' or 'top scorer'.
  • 为文颇驰骋波澜 - 'His writing was expansive and full of emotional or stylistic variation'; metaphor for a dynamic or expressive writing style.
  • 性疎放 - 'His nature was unrestrained or free-spirited'; common way to describe a person's personality in classical biographies.
  • 嗜酒不拘细事 - 'Loved wine and was not concerned with trivial matters'; common characterization of a free-spirited scholar or official.
  • 初对廷策 - 'At first, he presented his response to the imperial examination questions in court'; refers to the process of answering questions posed by the emperor.
  • 特授太子副司经国史院编修官 - 'Was specially appointed as an assistant compiler in the Imperial Academy for the Crown Prince'; specific title and position in the imperial court.
  • 进司经 - 'Promoted to the position of compiler in the Imperial Academy'; promotion within the court's scholarly bureaucracy.
  • 末帝在东宫颇见知 - 'When the last emperor was in the East Palace (i.e., as Crown Prince), he was well known and favored'; refers to the emperor's early life and political career.
  • 出为平凉府治中 - 'Was appointed as the governor of Pingliang Prefecture'; common way to describe a local government post.
  • 入为待制 - 'Was recalled to serve as a literary official in the imperial court'; '待制' was an honorific title for scholars in the court.
  • 出刺州未赴南京被围 - 'Was appointed as governor of a state, but before he could take up the post, Nanjing was surrounded'; reflects a common narrative of political turmoil.
  • 食乏服絶粒药 - 'His food ran out, and he took medicine to fast'; description of a physical or health condition.
  • 俄饮酒被药死 - 'Soon after, he drank alcohol and died from the medicine'; implies that the medicine may have been lethal or reacted with the alcohol.
  • 赋吕唐卿海藏斋诗 - 'Composed a poem about the Sea-Store Room (Hai Cang Zhai) of Lu Tangqing'; reference to a poem written about a scholar's study or collection.
  • 虚白云中含法界 - A poetic line; '虚白云' may refer to a cloud or a metaphor for the void or emptiness, while '法界' refers to the realm of Dharma or Buddhist cosmology.
  • 软红尘底寄虚舟 - Another poetic line; '软红尘' refers to the mundane world or worldly life, while '虚舟' is a metaphor for a vessel or journey in emptiness.
  • 只应乌帽红尘底 - Another poetic line; '乌帽' refers to a black hat, a symbol of scholar-officials, and '红尘' again refers to the mundane world.
  • 羞见苍烟白鹭洲 - Another poetic line; '苍烟' refers to the mist or haze of the sky, and '白鹭洲' is a poetic name for a place or a metaphor for a remote or peaceful land.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 彼国主之事尚不得见况其臣乎 - Rhetorical question meaning 'If the matter of the sovereign cannot be seen, how much less so the subject?' Uses the structure *况...乎* to emphasize a lesser case in contrast to a greater one.
  • 改朝 - To change the court (i.e., change the time of the day for court proceedings), used in the context of a solar eclipse.
  • 罢朝 - To cancel the court session, often due to adverse weather or other reasons.
  • 四不朝之二 - Refers to the four cases of not attending court mentioned in *Zengzi’s Questions* (《曾子问》), and here refers to the second of those four.
  • 典制 - Refers to established rituals, regulations, or court procedures.
  • 脱漏 - A term meaning omission or missing entries, used here in reference to gaps in historical records.
  • 右见 - A term used to indicate that what follows is found in a specific source or record, often used in historical texts.
  • 神道碑 - A type of memorial stele or inscription commemorating the virtues and achievements of a deceased person, often erected by family or disciples.
  • 清州防御使 - A military title, meaning the governor or commander of the defense forces in Qingzhou.
  • 钦定四库全书 - The *Four Treasuries*, a massive imperial compendium of Chinese literature compiled under the Qing dynasty.
  • 少擢第入仕 - A common biographical phrase meaning 'was selected early in the imperial examinations and entered official service.'
  • 有能名 - A phrase meaning 'had a reputation for ability.'

  • 金史世宗本纪大定十六年 - From the *Jin Shi* (History of the Jin Dynasty), the biography of Emperor Shizong (世宗), the 16th year of the Dading era (大定十六年).
  • 万春节 - A festival celebrating the emperor’s birthday, common in Chinese dynasties.
  • 日食改朝 - In the event of a solar eclipse, court proceedings were suspended, and the time of the court was changed (i.e., postponed).
  • 大雨罢朝 - In the case of heavy rain, court proceedings were canceled.
  • 曽子问四不朝之二 - Refers to *Zengzi’s Questions* (《曾子问》), a text from the Analects tradition, which lists four situations in which a subject might not attend court. Here, the second of those four is being referenced.
  • 周煇北辕录 - A historical record by Zhou Hui, documenting events of the Southern Song dynasty.

  • 钦定四库全书 - The *Four Treasuries* (四库全书), the largest imperial collection of Chinese texts compiled during the Qing dynasty.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 李左丞蹊字贯之大兴人 - Left Chancellor Li (李左丞). The title *左丞* refers to the Left Chancellor of the Jin dynasty. '字贯之' means 'Style name *Guanzhi*' (貫之), a common practice in Chinese naming where a person receives a 'style name' (字) in addition to their given name. '大兴人' means 'From Daxing,' a place name in northern China, likely referring to Daxing District near modern Beijing.
  • 富察和卓 - A name of a high-ranking official. Note: '案富察和卓旧作蒲察合住今改' means 'According to the record, *FuCha He Zhuo* was previously written as *PuCha He Zhu* and has now been revised.' This indicates a change in the name from *蒲察合住* to *富察和卓*, likely a correction or adaptation to a later form of the surname.
  • 正大初 - 'In the early years of the Zhengda era,' referring to the reign title of Emperor Wanyan Shouxian (金宣宗) of the Jin dynasty, who ruled from 1214 to 1224.
  • 防知政事 - A title meaning 'Coadjutor in State Affairs' or 'Deputy Chancellor.' This was a high-ranking administrative position.
  • 哀宗本纪 - 'The biography of Emperor Aizong (哀宗) in the official history.' Emperor Aizong was the last emperor of the Jin dynasty.
  • 天兴元年 - 'The first year of the Tianxing era,' another reign title of Emperor Aizong (r. 1224–1234).
  • 左丞李蹊送曹王出质 - 'Left Chancellor Li Xī sent Prince Cao to be a hostage.' This refers to the practice of sending a prince or noble as a hostage to ensure peace or alliance with another power.
  • 以李焕飞语诏左丞李蹊户部侍郎杨慥下狱 - 'Because of false accusations by Li Huan, the emperor issued an edict to imprison Left Chancellor Li Xī and the Minister of Revenue Yang Zao.'
  • 古纳之变 - 'The Gu Na incident' or 'the Gu Na rebellion.' *古纳* is likely a place or event name, possibly related to the fall of a Jin stronghold during the Mongol invasions.
  • 末帝 - 'The last emperor,' referring to Emperor Aizong of the Jin dynasty.
  • 睢阳 - 'Suiyang,' a city in modern Henan Province, historically significant in Chinese warfare and resistance.

Proper Nouns

  • 李蹊 - Li Xī, a historical figure, the Left Chancellor of the Jin dynasty.
  • 富察和卓 - FuCha He Zhuo, a high-ranking official. Note: previously written as *蒲察合住* (*PuCha He Zhu*), now corrected.
  • 大兴 - Daxing, a place in northern China, likely near modern Beijing.
  • 正大 - Zhengda, a reign title of Emperor Wanyan Shouxian of the Jin dynasty.
  • 哀宗 - Aizong, the last emperor of the Jin dynasty.

  • 又喜凌侮使者 - '凌侮' means to bully or insult, and '使者' refers to envoys or messengers. The phrase suggests a recurring behavior of mistreating envoys.
  • 凡朝廷遣使者来必以酒食困之 - '酒食困之' is an idiom meaning to exhaust or overwhelm the envoy with alcohol and food, a form of psychological or physical pressure.
  • 或辞以不饮因并食不给使饿而去 - '不给使饿而去' means to leave the envoy starving and forced to leave, indicating a deliberate act of mistreatment.
  • 此易治耳趣命左右持艾炷来 - '易治耳' means 'easy to treat,' and '艾炷' refers to moxa cones used in traditional Chinese medicine. The phrase suggests a quick and easy treatment.
  • 又因防宴诸将并妻皆在座时共食猪肉馒首 - '防宴' refers to a banquet, and '诸将并妻皆在座' means that the generals and their wives were all present.
  • 有一将妻言素不食猪肉 - '素不食猪肉' means 'has never eaten pork,' indicating a personal dietary restriction.
  • 雅尔呼达趣左右易之须防食讫问曰尔食何肉 - '趣左右易之' means 'quickly have the attendants replace it,' and '须防食讫' means 'after the banquet is over.' The question '尔食何肉' is a rhetorical one, implying that the meat was not actually pork.
  • 其人大呕疾病数日 - '大呕疾病数日' means 'vomited severely and fell ill for several days,' indicating a physical reaction to the deception.

[104]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 任履真字子山 - A standard biographical formula: 'Ren Lüzhēn, style name Zishān.'
  • 读书喜杂学 - 'Studied extensively and had a fondness for miscellaneous learning.' Indicates a broad intellectual interest.
  • 深于医 - 'Profound in medicine' or 'deeply skilled in medical science.'
  • 又有善行邑人皆信之 - 'He also had virtuous conduct, and the people of his town all trusted him.' Emphasizes moral character and public trust.
  • 贞祐初召入太医院 - 'In the early years of the Zhenyou era, he was summoned to the Imperial Medical Bureau.' Zhenyou is a reign title of the Jin Dynasty.
  • 旋告归 - 'Soon he requested to return home.' A common phrase for resigning or returning from office.
  • 与闲闲屏山诸公及余先子善 - 'He was on good terms with the scholars Xianxian, Pingshan, and my late father.' Indicates personal relationships.
  • 主长葛簿修儒宫及太虚观 - 'Served as the magistrate of Chánggě and oversaw the restoration of the Confucian temple and the Taixu Monastery.' Indicates administrative and cultural roles.
  • 子山之力居多 - 'Zishān's efforts were the main factor.' Highlights his contribution.
  • 为医起人疾甚众 - 'He healed the illnesses of many people through his medical practice.' Emphasizes his success as a physician.
  • 既卒闲闲志其墓云 - 'After his death, Xianxian composed an epitaph for his tomb.' Indicates a posthumous tribute.
  • 张子和睢州考城人 - 'Zhang Zihé, native of Kǎochéng, Suīzhōu.' Another standard biographical formula.
  • 初名从正 - 'Originally named Congzhèng.' Indicates a name change.
  • 精于医贯穿难素之学 - 'Was highly skilled in medicine and thoroughly understood the teachings of the *Nanjing* and *Shuowen*.' Refers to classical medical texts.
  • 其法宗刘守真完素 - 'His methods followed those of Liu Shǒuzhēn and Wang Wensu.' Refers to famous medical scholars.
  • 药多用寒凉 - 'His prescriptions often used cold and cooling herbs.' A medical practice.
  • 起疾救死多取效 - 'He frequently succeeded in curing illnesses and saving lives.' Emphasizes his efficacy.
  • 士大夫称焉 - 'The scholar-officials praised him.' Indicates social recognition.
  • 为人放诞无威仪 - 'His personality was unrestrained and lacked formal dignity.' Describes his character.
  • 颇读书作诗嗜酒 - 'He read extensively, composed poetry, and loved drinking.' Indicates a cultured but eccentric lifestyle.
  • 久居陈游余先子门 - 'He lived long in Chén, and was a disciple of my late father.' Indicates a mentorship relationship.
  • 旋告去 - 'Soon he requested to leave.' Another instance of resigning or retiring.
  • 名重东州 - 'His fame was prominent in the eastern regions.' Indicates regional reputation.
  • 麻知防九畴与之善 - 'Má Zhīfáng Jiǔchóu was on good terms with him.' Indicates a personal relationship.
  • 使子和论说其术 - 'He had Zihé explain his methods.' Indicates a request for exposition.
  • 因为文之有六门三法之目 - 'Thus, he composed a text titled 'Six Categories and Three Methods.' Indicates a written work.
  • 将行于世 - 'Intended to be circulated in the world.' Indicates the work's purpose.
  • 防子和知防相继死 - 'After Zihé and Jiǔchóu died in succession.' Indicates a sequence of deaths.
  • 迄今其书存焉 - 'To this day, his book remains.' Indicates the survival of the work.
  • 僧徳普武川人 - 'Monk Dépǔ, native of Wǔchuān.' Another biographical formula.
  • 自号胜静老人 - 'Self-styled as Shèngjìng Elder.' Indicates a personal title.
  • 倜傥有机术 - 'He was bold and resourceful, with cleverness.' Describes personality traits.
  • 与士大夫游饮酒食肉豁如也 - 'He mingled freely with scholar-officials, drinking and eating meat without restraint.' Indicates a social and unorthodox lifestyle.
  • 在军中论兵 - 'He discussed military affairs in the army.' Indicates a military role.
  • 南渡居陈之开元寺 - 'After the southern migration, he lived in the Kaiyuan Temple in Chén.' Refers to the Jin Dynasty's southern relocation.
  • 尝着弥陀偈谈理性 - 'He once composed a hymn to Amitābha Buddha and discussed rationality.' Indicates religious and philosophical writings.
  • 先子为序之 - 'My late father wrote a preface for it.' Indicates a personal connection.
  • 屏山亦喜其俊爽不羁也 - 'Pingshan also admired his boldness and lack of restraint.' Indicates admiration.
  • 颇喜字画作诗 - 'He greatly enjoyed calligraphy and poetry.' Indicates artistic interests.
  • 年六十余死 - 'He died in his sixties.' Indicates age of death.
  • 余谓古之文畅秘演之流 - 'I say he was in the line of ancient scholars like Wénchàng and Mìyǎn.' Indicates comparison to historical figures.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 贞祐 - A reign title of Emperor Wanyan Liang of the Jin Dynasty (1214–1217).
  • 太医院 - The Imperial Medical Bureau, a government institution in the Jin Dynasty responsible for medical care and training.
  • 闲闲 - A style name, possibly referring to Zhao Mengfu (Zhao Shixiu), a famous scholar of the Jin and Yuan periods.
  • 屏山 - Another style name, possibly referring to Zhao Mengfu or another scholar.
  • 余先子 - 'My late father,' indicating the author's father, who was involved in Confucian and medical affairs.
  • 长葛簿 - The magistrate of Chánggě, a local official position.
  • 儒宫 - A Confucian temple or academy.
  • 太虚观 - A Taoist monastery or temple.
  • 刘守真完素 - Liu Shǒuzhēn and Wang Wensu (also known as Huo Wenxiu), two important medical scholars of the Jin and Yuan dynasties.
  • 难素 - Refers to the *Nanjing* (Classic of Difficulties) and *Shuowen* (Classic of the Pivot), classical medical texts.
  • 士大夫 - Scholar-officials, the educated elite of the time.
  • 放诞无威仪 - A description of a person who is unconventional, lacking the formal dignity expected of scholars.
  • 陈 - A city, possibly referring to Chén in Henan, which was a significant cultural and administrative center during the Jin Dynasty.
  • 南渡 - Refers to the Jin Dynasty's migration to the south after the fall of the Northern capital, a historical event similar to the Southern Song's relocation.
  • 开元寺 - A Buddhist temple, likely referring to the Kaiyuan Temple in Chén.
  • 弥陀偈 - A hymn to Amitābha Buddha, a central figure in Mahayana Buddhism.
  • 理性 - Rationality or philosophical reasoning, possibly referring to Buddhist or Confucian rationalism.
  • 文畅秘演 - Wénchàng and Mìyǎn, ancient scholars known for their writings and philosophical insights, likely from the Tang or earlier periods.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 僧圓基字子初姓田氏亦北人 - Compound sentence introducing a monk named Yuanji (also known as Zichu), whose family name is Tian, and who is from the north. Typical of Classical Chinese biographical introductions.
  • 雖為浮屠善與豪士游 - 'Although he was a monk, he was good at associating with influential people.' '浮屠' means 'monk,' and '豪士' refers to influential or powerful individuals.
  • 負其材略有握兵治民之意 - 'He had a talent and a slight inclination towards governing people and managing troops.' '負其材' means 'to have a talent,' and '握兵治民' refers to military and administrative skills.
  • 葢于僧者也 - 'He was somewhat unconventional for a monk.' The character '' (likely a typo or corrupted character) suggests that he deviated from the norm.
  • 嘗住持南京靜安寺 - 'He once served as the abbot of Jing'an Temple in Nanjing.' '住持' means 'to serve as the abbot.'
  • 以不檢去之 - 'He was dismissed due to his lack of discipline.' '不檢' means 'lack of discipline.'
  • 岘山厯嵩阳 - 'He lived in Xian Mountain and later in Songyang.' '厯' means 'to pass through' or 'to live in.'

[106]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 滾碎阑干角 - A phrase likely referring to something being broken or damaged, possibly in a dramatic or violent manner. Could be interpreted as 'shattering the corners of the railing' or 'breaking the railing into pieces.'
  • 多僻怪不可晓 - Means 'full of obscure and strange things that are hard to understand.'
  • 出天上何书 - Means 'where it comes from in the heavens; what book is it?'
  • 书名亦不可晓 - Means 'the name of the book is also not known.'
  • 麻知防独重之 - Means 'Ma Zhifang alone values it.'
  • 肮脏风仪古丈夫 - Means 'his manner is that of an ancient, dignified man.'
  • 天上月明当额孤 - Means 'the bright moon of heaven shines alone on his forehead.'
  • 布囊春醉酒钱粗 - Means 'in spring, he gets drunk and carries his money in a coarse cloth bag.'
  • 危楼试倚街头月 - Means 'he leans on the moon at the top of a tall building near the street.'
  • 应见潜飞入玉壶 - Means 'he should see the moon fly silently into a jade vase.'
  • 正大初余过郾诸公为召至索诗 - Means 'in the early years of Zhengda, I passed through Yan, and the scholars there invited me to compose poetry.'
  • 求韵立书辞亦不可晓 - Means 'they asked for a rhyme, and I wrote it down, but it is also not understood.'
  • 后因病卒 - Means 'later, he died of illness.'
  • 案大金国志遭乱为顺天军将领所得知其名议欲挈之北归馆于州之瑞云观明日见将领自言曰我不欲住君家瑞云观也越三日卒后有见之淮上者与此志可以攷异 - Describes the aftermath of the person's death. According to the *Ta Jin Guo Zhi* (History of the Great Jin Dynasty), he was captured by a general of the Shuntian Army during chaos, and the general wanted to take him north to the State of Ruyun Temple. The next day, the person told the general, 'I do not want to stay in your home, Ruyun Temple.' Three days later, he died. Later, someone saw him in Huai, and this account can be compared with the *Ta Jin Guo Zhi* to find discrepancies.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 滚碎阑干角 - This phrase is likely a metaphorical expression, possibly referring to a dramatic or violent event.
  • 正大初 - Refers to the early years of the Zhengda era, a period in Chinese history.
  • 郾 - Refers to the city of Yan, a historical location.
  • 麻知防 - A person's name, likely a scholar or poet.
  • 大金国志 - The *History of the Great Jin Dynasty*, a historical text.
  • 顺天军 - Refers to the Shuntian Army, a military force.
  • 瑞云观 - A temple or place of worship.
  • 淮上 - Refers to the area around the Huai River, a historical region.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • 滚碎阑干角其他多僻怪不可晓 - This sentence is elliptical and could be interpreted as 'the railing was shattered, and the rest is full of obscure and strange things that are hard to understand.'
  • 出天上何书书名亦不可晓或云为鬼物所凭 - This sentence is a series of clauses that could be read as 'where it comes from in the heavens; what book is it? The name of the book is also not known. Or it is said to be possessed by a ghost or spirit.'
  • 正大初余过郾诸公为召至索诗求韵立书辞亦不可晓 - This sentence is a complex sequence of events: 'in the early years of Zhengda, I passed through Yan, and the scholars there invited me to compose poetry. They asked for a rhyme, and I wrote it down, but it is also not understood.'
  • 案大金国志遭乱为顺天军将领所得知其名议欲挈之北归馆于州之瑞云观明日见将领自言曰我不欲住君家瑞云观也越三日卒后有见之淮上者与此志可以攷异 - This sentence is a long, complex description of events and can be broken down into several parts for clarity.

  • 瑞云观 - A temple or place of worship.
